title: coumarouna micrantha
intro: Coumarouna micrantha, also known as the Brazil nut tree, is a large evergreen tree native to the Amazon rainforest. The tree's seeds, commonly referred to as Brazil nuts, have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The seeds are rich in selenium, a vital mineral that helps protect cells from damage, and have been linked to various health benefits, such as improved immune function and reduced risk of certain cancers. Brazil nuts are typically consumed as a snack or used in herbal preparations, including teas and capsules, to support overall health and well-being.
